author: zlipeng
abstract: Parse Swagger 2.0 / OpenAPI 3.x JSON specs given either a direct api-docs URL or a Swagger UI URL, without loading the whole document into agent context. Uses jq + a local cache (~/.cache/swagger-skill/) so multi-megabyte specs with thousands of endpoints answer single-endpoint queries in milliseconds and return only a few KB. Ships five shell scripts: resolve.sh turns any input URL into a concrete api-docs JSON URL — for Swagger UI URLs it consults /v3/api-docs/swagger-config (Springdoc) or /swagger-resources (Springfox), uses the urls.primaryName query param to pick the right group, and also surfaces any tag / operationId hints encoded in the URL fragment; fetch.sh calls resolve.sh, downloads the spec, validates it as JSON, caches it, and emits a lightweight index of {method, path, summary, operationId, tags} per endpoint; list.sh browses that index with --tag/--method/--limit filters and table/json/paths formats; search.sh is a case-insensitive substring search over path/summary/operationId/tags; get.sh extracts a single operation by either <path> [--method M] or --op <operationId>, recursively inlining every $ref up to a configurable depth and handling both #/definitions/X (Swagger 2.0) and #/components/{schemas,parameters,responses,requestBodies}/X (OpenAPI 3.x). External $refs are left verbatim, cyclic schemas stop at the depth cap. The agent never reads the cached full spec directly. Triggered when the user supplies a swagger/openapi/api-docs JSON URL or a Swagger UI URL and asks to list, search, or inspect endpoints.
